title: IntelHub
abstract: A desktop tooling for technology watch and innovation tracking, enabling companies of any size to gain clarity on what initiatives are being pursued across the organisation.
why_we_care: Large companies struggle to know every initiative being done throughout the organisation, leading to duplicated efforts and missed synergies. Smaller companies often have no tooling at all to track their technology landscape and innovation pipeline. IntelHub addresses both ends of this spectrum by providing a lightweight, Git-backed desktop application that brings visibility and structure to technology intelligence and innovation progress.
summary: IntelHub is a demonstrative desktop application (Tauri v2 + SvelteKit) currently in the ideation phase. It provides two profiles: the IntelHub profile for technology watch (tracking tools, technologies, processes) and the InovHub profile for following status and progress of a set of initiatives. The tool is Git-backed and GitHub-integrated, making it easy to collaborate across teams. At this stage it does not include AI capabilities, but these could be added if users show interest in such tooling.
description:
IntelHub is an open-source desktop application built with Tauri v2 and SvelteKit, available at https://github.com/haal-ai/haal-intelhub. It operates with two distinct profiles:

**IntelHub profile** — Focused on technology watch. Teams track tools, technologies, and processes across a tech-radar-style visualisation with rings (Adopt, Trial, Assess, Hold) to reflect maturity and recommendation level.

**InovHub profile** — Focused on innovation pipeline. Teams track initiatives through an innovation funnel: Ideation, POC (Proof of Concept), POV (Proof of Value), and MVP (Minimal Shippable Product), each with phase-specific rings reflecting progress.

Key characteristics:
- Git-backed: all data lives in a Git repository, providing full history and collaboration via pull requests.
- GitHub-integrated: discussions, issues, and news are pulled from the GitHub repository.
- Multi-hub: users can follow multiple hubs simultaneously and switch between them.
- Low barrier: no server infrastructure needed — just clone and go.

This initiative is currently in the ideation phase. A demonstrative version of the tooling has been realised to validate the concept. No AI capabilities are included at this stage, but the architecture is designed to accommodate them if user interest is demonstrated.

The expected impact on any company is low at this stage — the primary benefit being clarity of technology watch status and innovation progress tracking.
